chosen_action.title: Reconcile post-hold release scheduling against live cron state before re-entry

why_this_action.summary: The post-hold execution board was still empty, no truthful fresh external/manual lane remained, and the release-run path was relying on historical log state instead of the live cron scheduler. That made stale scheduled reruns look real and left the next executable slot vulnerable to duplicate or missing post-hold release jobs.
shared_findings_used: drafts/post_hold_distribution_reentry_latest.md: if the execution board is still empty after hold clears, do a concrete runtime/process repair instead of another measurement_hold
agents/marketing/logs/adoption_metrics_latest.md: Codeberg remains flat and fake progress should not be rewarded
agents/marketing/logs/distribution_lane_latest.json: current lane is distribution_architecture_repair, not another hold
agents/marketing/logs/marketing_workflow_audit_latest.md: repair the process in the same run when the architecture is too weak to improve outcomes
drafts/marketing_execution_board_latest.md: no truthful do-now handoff packet is currently available
openclaw cron list --json: the real scheduler state is the source of truth for whether a post-hold rerun actually exists

result.summary: Patched measurement-hold release scheduling so the marketing runtime checks live cron jobs first, removes stale pending release jobs before creating a new one, and only surfaces post-hold reruns that actually exist in the scheduler. Added regression coverage and verified the full measurement-hold executor suite.
